title: Use conmon pidfile in generated systemd unit as PIDFile.
body:
By default, `podman` points `PIDFile` in generated unit file to non-existent file (`$cid.pid`). As a result, the unit file, generated by `podman generate systemd`, is broken: an attempt to start this unit without prior modification results in a crash, because `systemd` can not find the pidfile of service's main process:

```
podman create --name sleeper alpine sleep 1000
podman generate systemd sleeper > ~/.config/systemd/user/sleeper.service
systemctl --user start sleeper

<crash, check the logs>

sleeper.service: Can't open PID file /home/qazer/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs-containers/0e2a3a918e0c48d3962e15a77c2eeb006190369bb5419c35ed78951d47123e80/userdata/0e2a3a918e0c48d3962e15a77c2eeb006190369bb5419c35ed78951d47123e80.pid (yet?) after start: No such file or directory
```

It would be logical to have an invariant "a podman-generated unit from existing container should succesfully run without modifications". This PR changes the generation process so that this invariant is satisfied and is checked by new system test.

comments.body:
Hi @QazerLab. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`
Probably worth adding a default conmon pidfile path, so we can use `generate systemd` on containers created with default options.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon*, *QazerLab* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Alternatively... `conmon` is guaranteed to make a PID file for the container, at a path designated by https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/libpod/oci_linux.go#L225

We can expose the path with a function and use it in `generate systemd`

> Alternatively... `conmon` is guaranteed to make a PID file for the container, at a path designated by https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/libpod/oci_linux.go#L225
> 
> We can expose the path with a function and use it in `generate systemd`

I am not sure that would work.  conmon is already waiting for that PID.

> We can expose the path with a function and use it in `generate systemd`

This _seems_ to work in practice, but I not sure that completely correctly, as `systemd` issues a warning if you try to make it supervise not an immediate child (guess this is because in this case `systemd` will not to receive `SIGCHLD` for specified PID on its death). For non-child processes, `systemd` polls the process state from `/proc` as a workaround, and this is probably not so reliable (e.g. a different process with same (reused) PID may kick in after container death between polls).

I think that waiting for `conmon` is a more correct approach here, and we probably rather should make sure that `conmon` always has a pidfile (e.g. set a default location), as @giuseppe proposed.
Should be good to land once we can get the tests green.

@mheon, I fixed the test which constantly failed, but looks like there are some network-related failures in other checks for last commit. Could you rerun them? I don't think that they are related to the changeset.
